name: chart/gauge
description: A speedometer gauge — a value arc over a min–max sweep with threshold zones, ticks, and a needle.

meta.name: Gauge
meta.description: A speedometer gauge — a value arc over a min–max sweep with threshold zones, ticks, and a needle.

props.name: value
props.description: The value the needle points to.

props.name: thresholds
props.description: Coloured zones from min upward; the last should reach max.

props.name: sweep
props.description: Total sweep angle in degrees (270 = a speedometer arc).

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

props.name: plain
props.description: Marks only — no ticks/labels. For micro/inline charts.

examples.title: Speedometer with zones
examples.code:
import { Gauge } from '@cascivo/charts'

<Gauge
  title="CPU load"
  value={72}
  unit="%"
  thresholds={[
    { upTo: 50, color: 'var(--cascivo-chart-2)' },
    { upTo: 80, color: 'var(--cascivo-chart-3)' },
    { upTo: 100, color: 'var(--cascivo-chart-4)' },
  ]}
/>

whenToUse: A single value against a range with a needle and threshold zones
Dashboard KPIs where a speedometer metaphor aids quick reading
whenNotToUse: A simple bar against thresholds — use Meter

related.name: Meter
related.reason: Use a linear bar for a single value against thresholds

related.name: RadialBar
related.reason: Use concentric rings for multiple progress values

intent.a11yRationale: Renders with role="img"; the value and range are in the fallback table.
